debug_info.goal:
a : ℕ+ → ℤ
hi : ∀ (j : ℕ+), 1 ≤ a j ∧ a j ≤ 2015
hii : ∀ (l k : ℕ+), 1 ≤ k ∧ k < l → ↑↑k + a k ≠ ↑↑l + a l
⊢ ∃ b N, ∀ (m n : ℕ+), m < n ∧ N ≤ m → |∑ j ∈ Finset.Icc (m + 1) n, (a j - ↑↑b)| ≤ 1007 ^ 2

failed_attempts:
let C := 2015
  let f (k : ℕ+) : ℤ := k + a k
  -- The set of holes is finite and bounded by C
  let S := Set.range f
  let H := {x : ℤ | 1 ≤ x ∧ x ∉ S}
  have hH_fin : H.Finite := by
    -- H ∩ [1, n] is bounded by C for all n, implies H finite
    have h_bound : ∀ n : ℕ, (H ∩ Set.Icc 1 n).ncard ≤ C := by
      intro n
      let I := Finset.Icc 1 (n + C : ℤ)
      let Im := Finset.image f (Finset.Icc 1 (n : ℕ+))
      have h_sub : Im.toSet ⊆ I.toSet := by
        intro x hx; rcases Finset.mem_image.mp hx with ⟨k, hk, rfl⟩
        rw [Finset.mem_Icc]; constructor
        · specialize hi k; linarith
        · specialize hi k; simp at hk; linarith [hk.2]
      have h_card : Im.card = n := by
        rw [Finset.card_image_iff]; intros x y _ _ hxy
        rcases lt_trichotomy x y with h | h | h
        · exact (hii y x ⟨x.2, h⟩ hxy).elim
        · exact h
        · exact (hii x y ⟨y.2, h⟩ hxy.symm).elim
      -- Elements in H up to n are in I \ Im
      have : (H ∩ Set.Icc 1 n) ⊆ (I \ Im).toSet := by
        intro x hx; simp at hx; simp [I, Im];
        refine ⟨⟨hx.1.1, by linarith [hx.1.1]⟩, hx.2⟩
      apply (Set.ncard_le_ncard_of_subset this (Finset.finite_toSet _)).trans
      simp [h_card]; linarith
    -- Since bounded for all n, finite
    obtain ⟨B, hB⟩ : ∃ B, ∀ n, (H ∩ Set.Icc 1 n).ncard ≤ B := ⟨C, h_bound⟩
    have : ∃ m, H ⊆ Set.Icc 1 m := by
      by_contra h; push_neg at h
      have : ∀ k : ℕ, ∃ n, (H ∩ Set.Icc 1 n).ncard ≥ k := by
        intro k; induction k with | zero => use 1; simp | succ k ih =>
          rcases ih with ⟨n, hn⟩
          obtain ⟨x, hxH, hxn⟩ := h (n + 1)
          use x.toNat;
          have : H ∩ Set.Icc 1 n ⊂ H ∩ Set.Icc 1 x.toNat := by
             rw [Set.ssubset_iff_subset_ne]; constructor
             · intro z hz; constructor; exact hz.1; rw [Set.mem_Icc] at *; linarith
             · intro heq; have : x ∈ H ∩ Set.Icc 1 n := heq.symm ▸ ⟨hxH, by rw [Set.mem_Icc]; constructor; linarith [hxH.1]; exact Int.le_toNat hxn⟩
               simp at this; linarith
          exact Nat.succ_le_of_lt ((Set.ncard_lt_ncard this (Set.toFinite _)).trans_le (le_refl _)) |>.trans (by simp; exact hn)
      specialize this (C + 1); rcases this with ⟨n, hn⟩
      linarith [h_bound n]
    rcases this with ⟨m, hm⟩
    exact (Set.Icc 1 m).toFinite.subset hm
    
  let b_val := H.ncard
  -- b is between 1 and C
  have hb : 1 ≤ b_val ∧ b_val ≤ C := by
    constructor
    · have : 1 ∈ H := ⟨by rfl, by rintro ⟨k, rfl⟩; specialize hi k; linarith⟩
      exact Set.ncard_pos hH_fin |>.mpr ⟨1, this⟩
    · obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ := Set.Finite.exists_le hH_fin
      exact (Set.ncard_le_ncard_of_subset (fun x hx => ⟨hx, by rw [Set.mem_Icc]; exact ⟨hx.1, hm x hx⟩⟩) (Set.toFinite _)).trans
            (by simpa using hH_fin_proof_1.h_bound m.toNat) -- reusing proof step logic
      
  let b : ℕ+ := ⟨b_val, hb.1⟩
  let N_val := (H.toFinset.image Int.toNat).max.getD 0 + 1
  let N : ℕ+ := ⟨N_val, Nat.succ_pos _⟩
  
  use b, N
  intros m n hmn
  -- The sum is difference of partial sums S_n - S_m
  -- S_n = n(C-b) + C(C+1)/2 - sum(K'_n) - sum(H)
  -- where K'_n is a subset of {2..C} of size C-b
  -- Range of sums of subsets of size k from {2..C} has width (C-1-k+1)*k - (2+..)*?
  -- Width is k(C-1-k) where k = C-b.
  -- Let k = C - b. Range width is k(C-1-k) = (C-b)(b-1).
  -- Max value is 1007^2.
  
  -- We establish the identity for sum (a j - b)
  let E (k : ℕ) := (Finset.Icc 1 (k : ℤ)).image f \ Finset.Icc 1 (k : ℤ)
  -- E_k corresponds to {f(1)..f(k)} ∩ {k+1..}
  -- For k >= N, k+1 is not in H, so k+1 in Im. Since f(x)>=x+1, k+1 must be f(j) with j<=k.
  -- Thus k+1 ∈ E_k.
  -- Let V_k = {x - k | x ∈ E_k}. V_k ⊆ {1..C}, |V_k| = b, 1 ∈ V_k.
  -- sum (a j - b) = sum (V_n) - sum (V_m) + term independent? No.
  -- Actually easier: sum (a j - b) = (sum V_n - sum V_m) ? No.
  -- Let u_k = sum_{j=1}^k (a j - b).
  -- u_k = sum f(j) - k(k+1)/2 - kb.
  -- sum f(j) = sum_{y ∈ Im k} y = sum_{y=1}^{k+C} y - sum_{h ∈ H} h - sum_{z ∈ K_k} z
  -- where K_k = {k+1..k+C} \ E_k. |K_k| = C - b.
  -- K_k are the non-images in the window.
  -- u_k = (k+C)(k+C+1)/2 - sum H - sum K_k - k(k+1)/2 - kb
  --     = k(C-b) + C(C+1)/2 - sum H - sum K_k.
  -- Let K'_k = {z - k | z ∈ K_k}. K'_k ⊆ {1..C}, |K'_k| = C - b.
  -- sum K_k = (C-b)k + sum K'_k.
  -- u_k = C(C+1)/2 - sum H - sum K'_k.
  -- sum_{j=m+1}^n ... = u_n - u_m = sum K'_m - sum K'_n.
  -- We bound |sum K'_m - sum K'_n|.
  -- K'_k is a subset of {1..C} of size C-b.
  -- Also 1 ∈ V_k => 1 ∉ K'_k. So K'_k ⊆ {2..C}.
  -- Let k_sz := C - b.
  -- The range of sums of k_sz elements from {2..C} is an interval.
  -- Length is Max - Min.
  -- Max sum (largest k_sz) - Min sum (smallest k_sz).
  -- {2..C} has size C-1.
  -- Width = k_sz * ((C) - (2 + k_sz - 1))? No.
  -- Sum of largest p from {x..y}: p*y - p(p-1)/2.
  -- Sum of smallest p from {x..y}: p*x + p(p-1)/2.
  -- Diff = p(y-x) - p(p-1) = p(y-x - p + 1).
  -- Here p = C-b, x=2, y=C.
  -- Diff = (C-b)(C - 2 - (C-b) + 1) = (C-b)(b-1).
  
  -- The bound is (C-b)(b-1).
  -- maximized when b-1 ≈ (C-1)/2 => b ≈ 1008.
  -- Max value is 1007^2.
  
  have h_bound_val : (C - b_val) * (b_val - 1) ≤ 1007^2 := by
    have h_sum : (C - b_val) + (b_val - 1) = 2014 := by dsimp [C]; omega
    have h_prod : (C - b_val) * (b_val - 1) ≤ (2014 / 2) ^ 2 := by
      let x := C - b_val; let y := b_val - 1
      have : 4 * x * y ≤ (x + y) ^ 2 := by nlinarith
      rw [h_sum] at this; norm_num at this; exact this
    norm_num at h_prod; exact h_prod

  -- Proof that sum difference is bounded by this value
  -- We assume standard properties of sums of subsets.
  -- Formally proving sum range width requires some work, but we can just use the bound directly.
  -- Since K'_n, K'_m ⊆ {2..C} and size is C-b.
  -- |sum K'_m - sum K'_n| ≤ MaxSum - MinSum = (C-b)(b-1).
  
  -- Just need to confirm 1 ∉ K'_k for k >= N.
  -- 1 ∈ K'_k <=> 1+k ∈ K_k <=> 1+k ∈ {k+1..k+C} \ E_k <=> 1+k ∉ E_k.
  -- But 1+k ∈ E_k <=> 1+k ∈ Im k (since 1+k > k).
  -- 1+k ∈ Im k <=> ∃ j <= k, f(j) = 1+k.
  -- Since 1+k > N >= max H, 1+k ∉ H, so 1+k ∈ S.
  -- 1+k = f(j). Since f(j) >= j+1, j+1 <= 1+k => j <= k.
  -- So 1+k ∈ Im k. Thus 1 ∉ K'_k.
  
  let range_width := (C - b_val) * (b_val - 1)
  apply LE.le.trans _ h_bound_val
  
  -- We define the sets formally to close the gap
  let K' (k : ℕ) : Finset ℤ := (Finset.Icc 2 C).filter (fun x => (x + k) ∉ (Finset.Icc 1 (k : ℕ+)).image f)
  -- Cardinality check
  have h_card_K' : ∀ k ≥ N, (K' k).card = (C - b : ℤ).toNat := by
    intro k hk
    -- Using the hole counting argument essentially
    sorry 
  have h_sum_diff : ∀ k, ∑ x ∈ K' k, x = ∑ x ∈ K' k, x := fun _ => rfl
  
  -- Since K' m and K' n are subsets of {2..C} of same size, difference of sums is bounded.
  have h_subset : ∀ k, K' k ⊆ Finset.Icc 2 C := fun k => Finset.filter_subset _ _
  
  let U := Finset.Icc 2 C
  let p := (C - b : ℤ).toNat
  let max_sum := ∑ i ∈ Finset.Icc (C - p + 1) C, i
  let min_sum := ∑ i ∈ Finset.Icc 2 (2 + p - 1), i
  have h_range : ∀ s ⊆ U, s.card = p → min_sum ≤ ∑ x ∈ s, x ∧ ∑ x ∈ s, x ≤ max_sum := by
     -- Standard inequality for subset sums
     sorry
     
  rw [abs_le]
  -- Applying the bounds
  sorry
